summary: Introduced Captain Falcon fighter decomp scaffolding by moving/splitting the old Falcon assembly into ftCaptain-specific asm files, adding a new ftCaptain header with CaptainAttr special-attribute layout, adding a small C file for Captain Falcon lifecycle/item/attribute callbacks, and updating obj_files.mk so the new C object and asm fragments replace the former monolithic ftfalcon.s object. The PR body says the work was done by Eric on Discord and adjusted by BR- to be acceptable for PR submission.
what_changed: Renamed/moved Falcon assembly into the character-specific path asm/melee/ft/chara/ftCaptain/ and split new assembly content across ftfalcon_1.s through ftfalcon_4.s.
Replaced the old obj_files.mk entry for asm/melee/ft/chara/ftfalcon.s.o with five objects: src/melee/ft/chara/ftCaptain/ftCaptain_1.c.o plus four ftCaptain asm fragment objects.

Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCaptain/ftCaptain.h with a CaptainAttr struct describing Captain Falcon special attributes, including Neutral-B, Side-B, Up-B, Down-B fields, and explicit UNK placeholders where meanings were not known.

Added C implementations/prototypes for early Captain Falcon callbacks including ftCFalcon_OnDeath, ftCFalcon_OnLoad, func_800E28E8, func_800E29C8, func_800E2A10, func_800E2A58, func_800E2AAC, and func_800E2B40.
The C code handles item pickup/catch/release/invisibility callbacks through held-item checks and fighter hand/hold animation helper calls such as func_80070FB4, func_80070C48, and func_80070CC4.
The OnLoad/reset-attribute code copies CaptainAttr data from ftData->ext_attr into x2D8_specialAttributes2 / x2D4_specialAttributes and sets fighter_data->x2224_flag.bits.b7.
Large remaining special-move code stayed as assembly, including functions around func_800E2B80 onward with action-state changes, effect pause/resume callbacks, ef_Spawn usage, and sdata2 constants.
smart_moves: Kept the build link order explicit by replacing one old Falcon asm object with the new C object followed by four asm objects in obj_files.mk, reducing the risk of accidental section/order drift.
Used a hybrid approach: decompiled the clearer lifecycle/item/attribute setup functions into C while preserving harder special-move behavior in assembly fragments.

Documented function roles in comments where known, such as OnDeath/OnRespawn, OnLoad, ResetAttribute, OnItemPickup/OnItemCatch, OnMakeItemInvisible, OnMakeInvisible, and OnItemRelease/unknown item-related behavior.
Left unknown attribute names as UNK or COMPLETELY_UNKNOWN rather than overclaiming semantic meaning.
Included decomp.me scratch links in comments for the translated C functions, giving future contributors matching context and provenance.
Used an existing fighter as a matching clue: the source comment says two unused locals in func_800E28E8 were identified by looking at Marth.
decomp_lessons: For fighter bring-up PRs, it is useful to create the character directory/header first, define the character special-attribute struct, and only translate the low-risk callbacks while leaving complex move logic in asm.
Special-attribute layouts can be made immediately useful even when incomplete by naming known fields semantically and preserving unknown fields with explicit UNK names.

OnLoad and ResetAttribute patterns commonly copy ext_attr into the fighter's per-instance special attributes; Captain Falcon follows that pattern through x2D8_specialAttributes2 and x2D4_specialAttributes.
Item callback matching can depend on exact unused locals or stack layout; comparing against another fighter implementation can reveal otherwise non-obvious matching artifacts.
Splitting a large asm file into ordered fragments can make incremental C replacement easier, but the object order in obj_files.mk becomes part of the matching strategy.
Evidence of review intent is weak for this PR: there were no review comments or issue comments in the provided context.
naming_conventions: Character directory uses ftCaptain even though the assembly basename remains ftfalcon_*.
New public header is named ftCaptain.h and uses the guard _ftcaptain_h_.
The character attribute struct is named CaptainAttr.
Known special-attribute fields use uppercase move-prefixed names such as NEUTRALB_FORWARD_MOMENTUM, SIDEB_SUCCESS_GRAVITY, UPB_LANDING_LAG, and DOWNB_GROUND_TRACTION.
Unidentified fields are retained with explicit uncertainty markers such as SIDEB_UNK1, UPB_UNK3, DOWNB_UNK2, and COMPLETELY_UNKNOWN.
Some callback names are semantic, e.g. ftCFalcon_OnLoad and ftCFalcon_OnDeath, while many still use address-based names such as func_800E28E8 and func_800E2B40.
Labels and undecompiled symbols in asm retain lbl_ and func_ address-style naming.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Preserved unconverted Captain Falcon code in asm/melee/ft/chara/ftCaptain/ftfalcon_1.s through ftfalcon_4.s rather than forcing speculative C.
Updated obj_files.mk to maintain ordered objects: ftCaptain_1.c.o followed by ftfalcon_1.s.o, ftfalcon_2.s.o, ftfalcon_3.s.o, and ftfalcon_4.s.o.
Annotated sdata2 labels in asm with float meanings such as 0.0f, 1.0f, 2.0f, 4.0f, and 0.01745329238474369f where visible in the diff.
C callback comments include decomp.me scratch URLs, indicating scratch-based matching workflow.
func_800E28E8 includes unused locals specifically noted as a matching discovery from Marth.
Assembly retained exact external call patterns for action-state changes and effects, including Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC, efLib_PauseAll, efLib_ResumeAll, ef_Spawn, sinf, and cosf.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or formal reviews were present in the provided PR context.
The PR body credits Eric in Discord and says BR- adjusted the work slightly to be acceptable, but no specific requested changes are documented in the provided dump.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename Captain Falcon func_800E28E8, func_800E2B40, or other ftCaptain address-based callbacks to semantic names.
Find later PRs that replace ftCaptain/ftfalcon_1.s through ftfalcon_4.s with C implementations.
Check subsequent changes to CaptainAttr field names to learn which UNK fields were later identified.
Search for fighter OnLoad/ResetAttribute implementations that copy ext_attr into x2D8_specialAttributes2 for reusable matching patterns.
Search Marth item callback implementations for the unused-local pattern referenced by func_800E28E8.
